jokes.text: Captain's log. Stardate, National Donut Day.
jokes.setup: Formal captain's log entry with official stardate format
jokes.punchline: Using a trivial food holiday as the official stardate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp Brannigan character moment - takes something serious (stardate) and makes it absurd. Economy is excellent, fits his pompous yet stupid persona.

jokes.text: Like that ball bearing I once mistook for a mint.
jokes.setup: Talking about weapons passing through fire ship
jokes.punchline: Random personal anecdote about confusing metal with candy
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp non-sequitur that reveals his stupidity through oversharing. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Pray fetch my travel tub. I do my best thinking in the shower.
jokes.setup: Facing military crisis requiring strategic thinking
jokes.punchline: Solution is to take a bath in the middle of battle
jokes.explanation: Sets up the shower sequence while showing Zapp's inappropriate priorities and self-indulgence.

jokes.text: Could you at least close the curtain, sir?
jokes.setup: Zapp taking public shower while humming
jokes.punchline: Kif's uncomfortable request for basic decency
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe comedy setup showing Kif's discomfort with Zapp's behavior.

jokes.text: You expect me to think without a loofah? Where's my loofah? You! You're the loofah.
jokes.punchline: Designating Kif as human loofah
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and character moment. Perfect Zapp logic - treating subordinate as object. Strong structure with the revelation.

jokes.text: Ah-ha! Water! Fire's mortal enemy!
jokes.punchline: Obvious solution presented as genius revelation
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp moment - treating obvious information like profound insight. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Your shower depleted the ship's three-year supply, sir.
jokes.setup: Questioning where all the water went
jokes.punchline: Zapp's shower used THREE YEARS of water supply
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke. The absurd scale (3 years!) delivered deadpan makes it very funny. Good structure.

jokes.text: I can end this battle with two words. 'We surrender.'
jokes.setup: Zapp claiming he can end battle with two words (expecting strategy)
jokes.punchline: The two words are surrender
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection structure and character moment. We expect tactical brilliance, get cowardice. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Sign this certificate of defeat, so I can show my mom.
jokes.punchline: Wants it as trophy for his mother
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Zapp's childish need for approval even in defeat. Original concept.

jokes.text: Big confident dot on the I.
jokes.punchline: Describing his signature style with pointless bravado
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment showing Zapp's ego even in mundane details. Decent character integration.

jokes.text: Oh-ho. You're juicier than I thought, Kif.
jokes.punchline: Sexual comment about Kif's 'juiciness' during scrubbing
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor that establishes harassment theme. Relies on gross-out factor rather than clever structure.

jokes.text: I have had it! I'm through putting up with your... crap!
jokes.punchline: His big explosion is still relatively polite ('crap')
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kif's breaking point while maintaining his mild nature.

jokes.text: It's pronounced compliment.
jokes.punchline: Zapp 'correcting' pronunciation to compliment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures Zapp's delusional self-regard. Excellent economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Do you think it's me?
jokes.setup: Award for employee who won every previous month
jokes.punchline: Fry genuinely wondering if he might win
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry obliviousness. Simple but effective character humor.

jokes.text: You've hit rock top!
jokes.punchline: Malapropism mixing 'rock bottom' with 'top'
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism that accidentally makes sense. Decent wordplay with good economy.

jokes.text: A recurring dream. That I can't wake up from. Who wants cake? There's one handful left. I'll have it.
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's a nightmare, then immediately takes the last cake
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from dream to nightmare, plus selfish cake grab shows her frustration.

jokes.text: Who wants cake? There's one handful left. I'll have it.
jokes.setup: Polite offer to share remaining cake
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately claims it for himself
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender selfishness - character-consistent but predictable

jokes.text: I say, I say, present!
jokes.punchline: Foghorn Leghorn-style lawyer appears
jokes.explanation: Fan-service callback to recurring character. Relies on familiarity rather than fresh humor.

jokes.text: You're representing both sides in this case? Till my wings give out, Your Honor. I'm going to allow this.
jokes.setup: Lawyer representing both plaintiff and defendant
jokes.punchline: Judge allows obvious conflict of interest
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist legal humor. The casual acceptance of corruption is funny.

jokes.text: It was a joke. Literally everyone was laughing except Kif.
jokes.punchline: Admits the victim wasn't laughing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Zapp's obliviousness to others' feelings. Self-defeating defense.

jokes.text: That was just locker room talk. It was a Halloween costume. It was a different time! It was yesterday!
jokes.punchline: Claims 'different time' defense for yesterday
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of weak excuses culminating in the absurd 'yesterday was a different time.' Great structure.

jokes.text: He made me watch him perform an erotic fan dance with a ping-pong paddle and a no-show sock. - It was a different time! - It was yesterday!
jokes.setup: Zapp claims his behavior was from different era
jokes.punchline: Kif reveals it happened yesterday
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect timing - absurd image combined with deflation of Zapp's excuse

jokes.text: So you admit it! Your Honor, I have reached a verdict. I find my client, Zapp Brannigan, guilty.
jokes.punchline: Immediately finds his own client guilty
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist legal humor. Lawyer's incompetence reaches new heights.

jokes.text: Why are you massaging me? You just seem so tense. Also, you should smile more. You're prettier when you smile.
jokes.punchline: Zapp harassing the judge mid-sentencing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Zapp can't help himself even during his own trial. Classic harassment behavior.

jokes.text: Dating a woman over 35?
jokes.punchline: Zapp's guess reveals his shallow, ageist worldview
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Zapp's shallow values. Dark humor about aging.

jokes.text: The second-worst punishment.
jokes.punchline: Still considers dating older women worse
jokes.explanation: Callback that reinforces character consistency. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Frivolous poultry? You insult me, sir!
jokes.punchline: Chicken lawyer hears 'frivolous poultry' and takes offense
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works perfectly with the chicken character. Good structure and character integration.

jokes.text: So you're gonna be Kif's commanding lobster?
jokes.punchline: Fry says 'lobster' instead of 'officer'
jokes.explanation: Simple malapropism typical of Fry's character but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Psst. Stinky needs changing.
jokes.punchline: Reveals he has baby that needs diaper change
jokes.explanation: Character development humor - shows Kif's domestic responsibilities

jokes.text: I, Bender, humbly accept this prestigious appointment. Also, it comes with double salary.
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals mercenary motivation
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender moment - fake humility followed by greed. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Next item of business. We have... one, two, three office chairs for sale.
jokes.punchline: Immediately selling their furniture
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor showing Professor's callousness. Perfect timing and character integration.

jokes.text: I'm here for the sensitivity training, and, uh, also, do you have a better-looking sister?
jokes.punchline: Immediately hits on the receptionist
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment showing he learned nothing. Character integration is excellent.

jokes.text: Indecent exposure! You all saw it, right? You wanna see it again?
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's in sensitivity training
jokes.punchline: Offers to repeat the offense
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing character hasn't learned from consequences.

jokes.text: I could barely see it the first time. I've seen brighter. Yeah, well, it's not about the brightness. It's about the color temperature!
jokes.punchline: Technical lighting discussion about genitals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist turn treating indecent exposure like a photography critique. Very original concept.

jokes.text: You're obviously not the type to waste food. You should talk, you family-sized bucket of lard-ass!
jokes.setup: Zapp defending someone who throws food
jokes.punchline: Immediately throws food while insulting them
jokes.explanation: Good ironic structure - defending someone then immediately doing the same behavior.

jokes.text: Wow, you're ugly.
jokes.punchline: Immediately insults instructor's appearance
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Zapp's complete lack of filter. Not particularly clever.

jokes.text: It feels like this! This isn't some woke-ass seminar!
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates with actual physical violence
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - we expect sensitivity but get brutality. Subverts expectations well.

jokes.text: Hm, never been there. Sounds exotic.
jokes.setup: Dr. Kind says 'set course for degradation'
jokes.punchline: Treating 'degradation' like a travel destination
jokes.explanation: Clever literalization of metaphor. Good deadpan delivery implied.

jokes.text: Also, we want to show we care about looking like we care about hiring women.
jokes.punchline: Admits it's performative diversity theater
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of corporate diversity initiatives. The meta-awareness makes it particularly cutting.

jokes.text: Wow, it's huge! It's like a Princess cruise ship with laser cannons!
jokes.setup: Seeing the Nimbus for first time
jokes.punchline: Comparing warship to cruise ship
jokes.explanation: Decent visual observation joke. The contrast between luxury and warfare is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: Dear Captain's Diary. If you're not me, don't read this. We're preparing to make first contact with a primitive civilization. Anyway, G2G. TTYL. PS, Fry looks cute.
jokes.punchline: Treats it like teenage diary with text speak and personal details
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's inexperience. The contrast between formal and casual is well done.

jokes.text: A bucket on a rope. Sorry you can't come, Bender. Your robot technology might scare them.
jokes.setup: Avoiding advanced technology to not intimidate aliens
jokes.punchline: Using primitive bucket while excluding robot
jokes.explanation: Good ironic logic - using stone-age technology while robot sits out. Solid visual gag.

jokes.text: Hope my robot technology isn't scaring you. And now, to stop it.
jokes.setup: Bender appearing despite being told to stay behind
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically 'stops' his technology by continuing to be robotic
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender defiance of orders. Character consistent but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: But now, we know intelligent life exists, flying among the stars in a bucket.
jokes.punchline: Impressed by bucket transportation method
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - aliens assume bucket represents peak technology. Well-structured.

jokes.text: They have fewer knobs, but not none!
jokes.punchline: Focused on sexual characteristics
jokes.explanation: Sets up alien culture's touchy nature but relies on innuendo rather than cleverness.

jokes.text: No kidding. You traveled in a bucket.
jokes.punchline: Alien deadpan acknowledgment of bucket travel
jokes.explanation: Simple callback with good deadpan delivery. Economy is excellent but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Brush harder, tubby! You can't yell at me. You're on your short arms. I yell at any height I want!
jokes.setup: Dr. Kind being abusive in simulation
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy about height and continuing abuse
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with some escalation but relies on crude humor rather than wit.

jokes.text: I'm going to take a shower... right here on the bridge!
jokes.punchline: Public shower mirrors Zapp's earlier behavior
jokes.explanation: Direct callback to opening scene but doesn't add much new. Structure is fine but lacks originality.

jokes.text: I've heard of air.
jokes.punchline: Fry's underwhelming response to air
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment with excellent economy. The understatement is beautifully crafted and character-specific.

jokes.text: Fresh from the hole.
jokes.setup: Showing off the Great Blow Hole
jokes.punchline: Innuendo in description of air source
jokes.explanation: Cheap innuendo joke that doesn't add much. Relies on crude humor rather than clever wordplay.

jokes.text: Get out of our air!
jokes.punchline: Aliens treat air space as private property
jokes.explanation: Absurdist property rights - treating atmospheric air as exclusive resource

jokes.text: That sounds like a really boring casino.
jokes.setup: Admiral comparing planet to slot machine paying out air
jokes.punchline: Leela's dry observation about air-gambling
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan observation that deflates the Admiral's excitement. Well-crafted with good economy.

jokes.text: Not a lotta air out there, and what we do have is, like, 50% burp.
jokes.punchline: Space air is half burp
jokes.explanation: Original and absurd concept that creates a funny sci-fi world-building detail. Good quotability.

jokes.text: Our captain will gladly engage in the knobbling. - I will? I'd rather not. I said knobble!
jokes.punchline: Leela reluctant, Fry aggressively volunteers her
jokes.explanation: Standard diplomatic awkwardness with sexual undertones

jokes.text: Let's find out. Man. It does stink. It's like rotten flesh floating in a puddle of vomit. You wish. It's a durian!
jokes.punchline: Durian fruit is somehow worse than rotten flesh and vomit
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure. The 'you wish' response to disgusting description is perfectly timed and original.

jokes.text: My God. It actually has stink lines.
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about cartoon visual convention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke about cartoon conventions. Very original and self-aware humor that works perfectly in animation.

jokes.text: Now, who's the fat, incontinent moron?
jokes.punchline: Repeating his own insults back
jokes.explanation: Simple callback but doesn't add much. Predictable structure and execution.

jokes.text: Hey, man, that's catchy! What song is that playing on the emergency siren?
jokes.punchline: Mistaking alarm for music
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing character's obliviousness to danger. Decent structure.

jokes.text: What song is that playing on the emergency siren? - It's the emergency siren! We're under attack!
jokes.punchline: It literally is the emergency siren, not music
jokes.explanation: Standard misunderstanding humor - character confusion during crisis

jokes.text: Jumping Judas. I've never seen a simulation so realistic. Is it 'okay' to hit on a simulation?
jokes.punchline: Still focused on inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment - even when learning important information, he's still thinking about harassment. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: It was a teachable moment.
jokes.setup: Tied up sensitivity instructor after hijacking ship
jokes.punchline: Describes assault as educational opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan euphemism for criminal behavior - fits character's twisted teaching methods

jokes.text: So, our ship was real? And the simulation was simulated?
jokes.punchline: Redundant description of simulation being simulated
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay about confusion between reality and simulation. Not particularly clever.

jokes.text: One. Enough to befoul this planet's air supply for centuries!
jokes.setup: Asked how many more durians he has
jokes.punchline: One durian can poison entire planet for centuries
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of durian's power to absurd proportions. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: What a wimp! Captains need to be ruthless. Women belong in the kitchen, fixing me a sweet durian pie. That's an offensive stereotype. Leela's terrible at baking.
jokes.punchline: Fry objects to stereotype but confirms the specific detail
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Fry defends against sexism but accidentally confirms specific insult. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: I now know there's a difference between right and wrong. One of them gets you canceled.
jokes.setup: Zapp claiming to have learned moral lessons
jokes.punchline: Only learned the pragmatic consequences, not morality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Zapp learned nothing about morality, only consequences. Perfect structure and economy.

jokes.text: I now know there's a difference between right and wrong. One of them gets you canceled.
jokes.setup: Zapp claims to have learned moral lesson
jokes.punchline: Only lesson is avoiding consequences, not actual morality
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Zapp learns wrong lesson from punishment, focusing on avoiding detection rather than moral behavior

jokes.text: Your eight-hour sensitivity training is complete. Please deposit me...
jokes.punchline: Recording continues despite speaker being dead/unconscious
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about automated systems continuing after disaster. Decent structure but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I declare the groping optional. Where do I sign?
jokes.setup: Leela offering to knobble despite durian smell
jokes.punchline: Alien makes harassment optional and wants to sign treaty
jokes.explanation: Good resolution showing respect improving diplomacy. Makes the earlier harassment themes pay off positively.

jokes.text: It's pronounced velour.
jokes.punchline: Corrects pronunciation to his favorite fabric
jokes.explanation: Perfect character callback to Zapp's velour obsession. Excellent character integration and quotability.

jokes.text: What do you think this is, the Peace Corps? Get out!
jokes.punchline: Immediately fires her for having morals
jokes.explanation: Good ironic structure showing military's lip service to morality. Well-crafted escalation.

jokes.text: What do you think this is, the Peace Corps? Get out!
jokes.setup: Praising Leela's refusal to shoot civilians
jokes.punchline: Immediately fires her for the same behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent institutional satire - military praising then punishing same moral behavior

jokes.text: No, you have something better. You have what it takes to be Leela.
jokes.setup: Leela feeling inadequate as DOOP captain
jokes.punchline: Fry's simple but meaningful compliment about being herself
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - Fry's genuine love cutting through complex situation

jokes.text: May I slurp your cheek clean with my tendrils? Eh, sure. And thanks for asking first.
jokes.setup: Alien asking permission for gross behavior
jokes.punchline: Leela appreciates consent despite disgusting action
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment showing lesson learned about consent. Connects to episode's harassment theme.

jokes.text: Ew, blech! But also yum.
jokes.setup: Being grossed out by alien tongue cleaning
jokes.punchline: Admits it actually tastes good despite disgust
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out comedy - conflicted reaction to unpleasant but tasty experience
